---
title: Ex.1 Parsing Protein Structures
format: html
Authors: "Marta Aransay, Carlota de la Concepción, Marta Rodrigo, Lucía Santos"
date: 04/03/2026
---

# Introduction

Structural bioinformatics use data resources, algorithms and tool to study, analyze, predict and understand biomacromolecular structures. The structural information with high resolution over the biological systems allows the scientific comunity to known its functions and the effects of modifications on it.

The proteins are macromoleculues which function is highly related with their structure. The structure of proteins is distinguisehed into four levels, which depends on folding. The folding is due by non-covalent interactions between the atoms of residues of the same molecule, intramolecular, or other molecule, intermolecular. The structural bioinformatics try to characterize 




## Objective


---

# Methods

## Data Collection
[Describir que los hemos sacado de lo que nos ha dado el profesor pero añadir algo más sobre las proteínas, de donde salen, info principal de la página de PDB, etc.]

RCSB-PDB (Research Collaboratory for Structural Bioinformatics Protein Data Bank, https://www.rcsb.org/) is an organization which holds a database of exprimentally determined, and also predicted, tridimensional structures of proteins, nucleic acids and macromolecular complexes. 

Every protein uploaded in this database is identified with an alphanumeric identifier of 4 characters long. This identifier is used to gain access to each protein's information.

The tridimensional structures analyzed in this project belongs to the Plasma Retinol-Binding Protein Precursor of *Homo sapiens*, the Carbonic Anhydrase of *Methanosarcina thermophila* and the Catalase of *Homo sapiens*  proteins whose PDB identifier are *1RBP*, *1QRE* and *1DGF*, respectively.

## Parsing PDB Files
[Explicar el objetivo de esta función y describir las partes más importantes del código. Incluso por simplificar se puede meter una tabla con metadato -> de donde se extrae -> parte del código que lo hace]

## Metadata Extraction

## Distance and Contact Map Calculations

[Primero hacer una introduccion a que es la distancia el mapa de contacto (por qué son útiles), meter alguna explicacion matemática, y luego lo mismo que antes, describir las partes más importantes del código.]

\[
d_{ij}^2 = (x_i - x_j)^2 + (y_i - y_j)^2 + (z_i - z_j)^2
\]

---

# Results

TABLA CON LOS RESULTADOS DE LAS DISTANCIAS Y CONTACTOS

---

# Discussion

---

# Conclusion

---

# Annexes

## Work Register

[Aquí básicamente pondría el paso a paso que hemos seguido en github, con los commits, etc. para que se vea el proceso de trabajo]
